# Contributor Code of Conduct

Anthale is committed to a professional, respectful, and harassment-free environment across `anthale-openapi` and related collaboration spaces.

## Our Standards

Examples of behavior that contribute to a positive environment include:

- Showing respect and empathy toward other participants.
- Using inclusive language.
- Accepting constructive feedback professionally.
- Keeping technical disagreement focused on the work rather than the person.
- Acting with care when discussing security, safety, and trust-sensitive topics.

Examples of unacceptable behavior include:

- Harassment, discrimination, or derogatory comments.
- Publishing another person's private information without explicit permission.
- Personal attacks, intimidation, or sustained antagonism.
- Trolling, bad-faith review behavior, or disruption of discussions and collaboration.

## Maintainer Responsibilities

Project maintainers are responsible for clarifying expected behavior and taking fair, appropriate action in response to behavior they judge inappropriate, threatening, offensive, or harmful.

Maintainers may remove, edit, or reject comments, commits, code, issues, pull requests, discussions, and other contributions that are not aligned with this Code of Conduct.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies in all repository spaces, including issues, pull requests, discussions, review threads, and associated collaboration channels. It also applies when an individual is representing Anthale or this repository in public contexts.

## Reporting

If you experience or witness unacceptable behavior, report it privately through the relevant GitHub reporting or maintainer contact channel available for this repository or the Anthale organization. Reports will be reviewed promptly and handled as confidentially as practical.

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org), version 2.1.
